headers.//: JS/CSS: must-revalidate every load. Quest Browser + Chrome heuristic caching otherwise pin old JS for hours after a deploy. no-cache allows the browser to store the file but forces a 304 round-trip, so steady-state cost stays minimal.

HTML entry-points: no-store. This is the SINGLE most important header — when a user clicks Publish Site, the new deployment must reach them on the very first request. no-store forbids the browser from keeping the HTML at all, so every load talks to the CDN and picks up the latest <script src> versions.
Same no-store rule for any explicit *.html path so /index.html, /foo.html etc. all bypass the browser cache.
